video_title: Iran Claims No Progress on US Talks Amid Lebanon Clashes
transcript: Are we making progress? That's the one question I hear every single day on this program. Are we making progress? Any signs of that this morning? Yeah, well, it's the Middle East. Is the right sentiment here. A lot of conflicting statements. We're still getting a lot of those. And the latest is still pretty confusing. What we know is that Israeli officials had met with Liberty's counterparts yesterday, and what they reached was essentially talks to for Hezbollah to stop its hostilities against Israel and Israel to do the same. So it's conditional. Israel did say that if Hezbollah stops its attacks against the northern part, then Israel would reduce its hostilities. But just about an hour, an hour and a half ago, the hostilities remain in the southern part of Lebanon, where Israel has its deepest incursion in about 25 years. And so we're still seeing that the Lebanese president coming out to local media and saying that we may see the truce come into effect in the next 24 hours. But then he also did say, now he's waiting to hear responses from different parties that may include Hezbollah. That may include Israel as well. President Trump on his own right, did say that we may see broader negotiations between Iran and the United States carrying on this weekend. But then he also said they may not. So we go back to this back and forth that we've been witnessing over the past couple of months or so since the initial ceasefire was put in place. Still wait and see mode. Still waiting to see if the truce does come into effect between Lebanon and Israel for the broader interim deal to start being negotiated.
